title: Character Consistency Engine
description: Develop a system to track and enforce logical, emotionally consistent character behavior across scenes. This includes preserving motivation, detecting contradictions, and optionally validating tone coherence across LLM responses. Future versions may support internal conflict modeling and cross-scene behavioral audits.

title: Image Generation Integration
description: Add optional support for image generation via local or API-driven models. Images are treated as non-critical 'fluff' and stored in each storypack’s `/images/` directory. The system should support pluggable image models, matching how LLM adapters work. Future support may include image memory tagging, automatic character/scene visualization, and CLI-triggered generation.

title: Narrative Heatmap Generator
description: Build a heatmap that visualizes character, location, and emotional 'activity' across the timeline. Useful for writers editing structure and for measuring pacing. Exposed via CLI or exportable CSV.

title: What-If Forking Engine
description: Enable non-destructive timeline forks from any scene. Allow users to explore alternate decisions and outcomes, generating new branches stored in the storypack under `/branches/` with metadata describing the divergence.

title: Emotion Drift Tracker
description: Track a character's emotional state over time and log the arc (e.g., joy → confusion → anger → resolve). Integrate into timeline tools and memory audit logs for post-play analysis and author editing.

title: Character Q&A Mode
description: Enable users to ask characters out-of-world questions using memory and motivations. Characters should respond as if interviewed, using internal beliefs and knowledge, not just improv. This enhances immersion and debugging of character logic.

title: Motivation-Driven Response Weighting
description: Integrate a lightweight pre-generation module that scores and optionally biases character responses based on their core motivations, emotional context, and recent memory. Helps reduce random or illogical dialogue while preserving character intent.

title: Behavioral Fingerprinting System
description: Assign each character a behavioral fingerprint representing their evolving traits (e.g. 60% loyal, 25% anxious, 15% impulsive). Update fingerprint over time and use it to influence tone, decisions, and conflict simulations.

title: Character Conflict Engine
description: Detect direct conflicts between character goals and motivations. Use this system to trigger tension scenes, simulate offscreen developments (e.g. betrayal, sabotage), or raise stakes in dialogue. Connect to memory and timeline for continuity.

title: Scene Risk Grading System
description: Introduce a system to classify scenes based on narrative intensity, tone, safety flags, and potential legal gray zones (e.g. violence, NSFW, power imbalance). This helps users prep content for export and filter downstream LLM routing if desired.

title: Optional Anonymous Story Analytics
description: Provide opt-in telemetry for anonymous story statistics: character usage, scene length, genre tags, tone shifts. Enables performance tuning, model feedback, and community heatmaps without compromising user privacy or story ownership.

title: Character Mutation Lifecycle
description: Introduce schema and system support for evolving character identity, including name changes, aliases, appearance shifts, and physical or psychological transformation. Changes should be tracked in the dynamic_profile section of the character file, logged as canonical events, and reflected in prompt generation. Identity changes should not break memory, timeline, or scene continuity.
